title: Nagsankar Mandir
tagline: Nagsankar Mandir — spiritual town of Assam

overview.short: The Nagsankar Temple is a shrine in Nagsankar Mouza near Sootea, to the east of Tezpur, in Sonitpur District of Assam.
overview.description: The Nagsankar Temple is a shrine in Nagsankar Mouza near Sootea, to the east of Tezpur, in Sonitpur District of Assam. The temple is believed to be built by King Narasankar of Nagakha in the 4th century.

topPlaces.name: Sootea
topPlaces.description: Sootea is located under Naduar revenue circle, under Tezpur sub-division, in Biswanath district, Assam state, India, formerly in the district of Sonitpur.

topPlaces.name: Jamugurihat
topPlaces.description: Jamugurihat is a town and a town area committee in Sonitpur district, in the Indian state of Assam. This is a newly planned city area by the Urban Development Authority of Assam.

topPlaces.name: Rangachakua
topPlaces.description: Rangachakua is a village and a central area for some villages situated in Sonitpur district, in the Indian state of Assam. It provide various economic, financial and organizational service to the surrounding Region having the only bank in the area, only Higher Secondary.

topPlaces.name: Gamiripal, Sonitpur
topPlaces.description: Gamiripal, also known as Hukai Gaon or Kumar gaon, is a village in Jamugurihat of Sonitpur district, in the Indian state of Assam.

items.note: Sootea is located under Naduar revenue circle, under Tezpur sub-division, in Biswanath district, Assam state, India, formerly in the district of Sonitpur.
Jamugurihat is a town and a town area committee in Sonitpur district, in the Indian state of Assam. This is a newly planned city area by the Urban Development Authority of Assam.

Rangachakua is a village and a central area for some villages situated in Sonitpur district, in the Indian state of Assam. It provide various economic, financial and organizational service to the surrounding Region having the only bank in the area, only Higher Secondary.
Gamiripal, also known as Hukai Gaon or Kumar gaon, is a village in Jamugurihat of Sonitpur district, in the Indian state of Assam.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nagsankar Mandir?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nagsankar Mandir is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Nagsankar Mandir?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jorhat Airport (117 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (116 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Nagsankar Mandir?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nagsankar Mandir at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Nagsankar Mandir?
faq.a: Stays in Nagsankar Mandir start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Nagsankar Mandir?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sootea, Jamugurihat, Rangachakua, Gamiripal, Sonitpur.
